title: dryopteris pachyrhachis var. straminea
intro: Dryopteris pachyrhachis var. straminea, also known as the strawy-leaved golden shield fern, is a variety of fern that belongs to the Dryopteris genus. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant properties, which are attributed to its rich content of flavonoids and saponins. The health benefits of Dryopteris pachyrhachis var. straminea include its potential to lower blood pressure, improve cardiovascular health, and act as an anti-cancer agent, among other benefits. In traditional herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat a range of health issues.
